Babel (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $34,302,837Details
International Box Office $97,818,375
Worldwide Box Office $132,121,212
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $31,482,595
Total $31,482,595
Further financial details...

Synopsis

An accident connects four groups of people on three different continents: two young Moroccan goatherds, a vacationing American couple, a deaf Japanese teen and her father, and a Mexican nanny who takes her young charges across a border without parental permission.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$389,351 (1.1% of total gross)
Legs:6.17 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:26.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:7 opening theaters/1,251 max. theaters, 8.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $61,367,429

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 27th, 2006 (Limited) by Paramount Vantage
November 10th, 2006 (Expands Wide) by Paramount Vantage
International Releases: December 14th, 2006 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: February 20th, 2007 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for violence, some graphic nudity, sexual content, language and some drug use
Running Time: 143 minutes
Award nomination: 2007 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama
Cast, crew, or production detail: Segments
Geographical setting: Morocco
Plot point: Accidental Death, Deaf, Dysfunctional Family, Immigration, Suicide, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Subgenre: Life Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Anonymous Content, Una Produccion De Zeta Film, Central Films
Production Countries: United States, Mexico, Morocco, Japan
Languages: English
